meta.reportLabel: Fri Report — suspected JPY AMBUSH intervention (~1% surge, 2:30am ET, biggest move since Apr 30) in the thin US-holiday tape; RBNZ Jul 8 FLIPPED to consensus HOLD (was ~70% hike); Brent <$71 glut deepens - CAD weakest; EZ flash core wrinkle (2.5% up) keeps a Sept ECB hike alive.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: Thin-tape intervention regime inside an oil-glut disinflation world. The flagged Friday window delivered: a suspected no-warning MoF ambush (~1% JPY surge at 2:30am ET, largest move since Apr 30) after Reuters revealed Tokyo's shift to unsignalled strikes - the short-JPY carry trade now faces invisible tape-bombs. Beneath it, the glut deepens (Brent <$71, Hormuz >10mn b/d, Iran 40mn+ bbl shipped) pinning CAD as weakest, while Warsh's hawkish hold (CPI 4.2%, ~80% priced zero '26 cuts) keeps USD firm despite the +57k NFP miss. RBNZ Jul 8 flipped to consensus HOLD; Doha talks paused Jul 4-9 for Khamenei's funeral.
meta.nextBigEvent: RBNZ Wed Jul 8 - flipped to consensus HOLD (was ~70% hike), surprise-hike tail makes NZD risk asymmetric UP; funeral-week oil headlines the rolling tail

dailyRead: The overnight headline is the yen: at ~2:30am ET in today's thin pre-US-holiday tape, JPY surged ~1% - the largest single move since the April 30 intervention - and markets suspect Tokyo's first no-warning AMBUSH strike (Reuters revealed the tactic shift this week; official silence is the point). USD/JPY had touched 162.66, weakest since 1986. The deeper regime is unchanged: the oil supply glut (Brent below $71, lowest since late February; Hormuz flows >10mn b/d, Iran 40mn+ bbl shipped post-blockade) pins CAD as the clear weakest, while Warsh's hawkish hold (CPI 4.2%, zero 2026 cuts ~80% priced) keeps the dollar firm despite the +57k NFP miss. Two forward shifts today: RBNZ Jul 8 has FLIPPED from ~70% hike to a consensus HOLD (banks now see hikes from Aug/Sep) - NZD-long expressions trimmed (NZD/CAD now rides the oil leg alone); and the EZ June flash hid a hawkish detail - core ROSE to 2.5% even as headline cooled to 2.8%, keeping a Sept ECB hike alive under the soft-EUR thesis. Cleanest trades remain the CAD shorts: USD/CAD, AUD/CAD, NZD/CAD longs; EUR/USD short at MEDIUM; the long-JPY core may finally have its re-load signal - add ONLY on confirmed intervention follow-through. Doha talks pause Jul 4-9 for Khamenei's funeral: a headline vacuum over every oil-linked position.

strength.tag: hawkish hold intact; NFP dented, didn't break
strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May, highest since Sep-25); June NFP +57k miss BUT unemployment FELL to 4.2% (participation-driven); Fed 3.50-3.75%, dots erased 2026 cuts and flag a possible hike; ~80% priced: zero cuts in 2026.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hawkish hold intact into the Jul 28-29 FOMC: the NFP miss pared the hike tail (Sept ~50/50) but a falling jobless rate + 4%+ CPI bar cuts entirely; Warsh's 130-word statement = deliberate hawkish minimalism. Risk: a SECOND soft labor print kills the hike tail and unwinds stretched USD longs. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May, up from 2.5%); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes); next BoE Jul 30 + MPR.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - the services re-acceleration keeps the hike-tail and both dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR round. Risk: a soft activity/labor wave re-splits the MPC. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%); headline 4.0% (RBA sees a 4.8% Q2 peak); unemp 4.5% loosening. RBA 4.35% (hiked May), held Jun; market prices ~4.70% by year-end.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Sticky trim + year-end pricing keeps an Aug 11 hike tail open; AUD capped sub-0.70 by the firm USD and the oil-glut hit to commodity beta. Risk: cool Q2 jobs/CPI or risk-off flushes the beta. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.drivers: CPI 3.1% Q1 (RBNZ sees >4% Q2, 4.3% Q3 peak); unemp 5.3% Q1 (edging down from 5.4%). RBNZ 2.25%, held since Nov; May was a 3-3 split decided by Governor Breman's casting vote.
strength.forward: ⚖️ FORWARD SHIFT: Jul 8 is now a consensus HOLD (was ~70% hike) - the oil slide gutted the cost-shock case and bank economists (ASB/Westpac/ANZ) see hikes from Aug/Sep instead; UBS warns of de-anchoring if they wait. Hawkish-hold tone likely. Risk: a surprise 25bp hike (half the MPC wanted it in May) squeezes NZD sharply higher. Next RBNZ Jul 8.

strength.tag: suspected ambush: +1% surge in thin tape
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed, lowest since Mar-22); headline 1.5%; unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1; 31-yr high), guides more hikes; USD/JPY touched 162.66 (weakest since 1986) before the overnight move.
strength.forward: 🦅↔ The spot game changed: a suspected no-warning AMBUSH (~1% JPY surge, 2:30am ET, biggest move since Apr 30) after Reuters revealed the tactic shift - shorts now face invisible tape-bombs, and the BoJ path (more hikes, medium-term overshoot) backs the destination. Risk: if it fades like the ¥11.7tn April round, 163+ resumes - add long-JPY only on follow-through. Next BoJ Jul 30-31.

strength.tag: flash 2.8% cooling BUT core up to 2.5%
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%; energy 8.7% from 10.8%, services 3.2% from 3.5%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11, first since 2023), meeting-by-meeting.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Two-handed flash: the cooling headline guts Jul 23 urgency (hold expected), but rising core keeps a Sept hike alive - the dovish EUR read is less clean than headline suggests. Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 tone on the core print squeezes EUR shorts. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); unemp 6.6%; May jobs +88k but flat since Jan, wages 3.0% cooling; technical recession. BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold (Jun 10), looking through energy.
strength.forward: 🔻 The oil GLUT is the story: Brent <$71 (lowest since late Feb) with Hormuz >10mn b/d and Doha progress erodes the energy-CPI floor while the terms-of-trade hit deepens; BoC sidelined Jul 15. Risk: funeral-week talks collapse / fresh Hormuz incident re-spikes oil and rescues the loonie. Next BoC Jul 15.

catalysts.note: The flagged window DELIVERED: a suspected MoF ambush at ~2:30am ET (~1% JPY surge); more possible into the close.
Both-ways oil tail: 'positive progress' claimed pre-pause, but Iran issued a fresh Hormuz warning Jul 2; a headline vacuum over every oil-linked position.
FLIPPED to consensus HOLD (was ~70% hike); the surprise-hike tail makes NZD risk asymmetric UP - the NZD event.
Whether May's +88k was noise; feeds BoC Jul 15.
The 4.2%-headline regime check - sets the FOMC hike-tail.
Hold; the oil glut + technical recession vs 3.2% CPI.
RBNZ expects >4% - validates or kills the Aug/Sep hike path.
Hold; watch the core-2.5% language - the Sept-hike signal.
Warsh round 2; hold + hawkish minimalism expected; Sept ~50/50.
7-2 hold expected; the dissent count is the GBP signal.
Hold 1.00%; guidance on further hikes + intervention coordination post-ambush.
Brent <$71 (lowest since late Feb); Iran 40mn+ bbl, record Russian exports, floating storage building - the CAD pain.
The no-warning regime is confirmed by Reuters sources - every thin tape is now live; do not carry short-JPY into thin sessions.

geopolitics: The war has moved from battlefield to funeral hall and negotiating table. Khamenei's state funeral runs July 4-9 (he was assassinated Feb 28 in the strikes that opened the war; successor Mojtaba Khamenei will reportedly skip it for security reasons) and the Doha indirect talks are PAUSED until it ends - after a week both sides called 'positive progress,' with Trump saying denuclearization is 'moving along well.' The market consequence: Brent broke below $71, its lowest since late February, because the supply picture has flipped from blockade to glut - Hormuz flows are back above 10mn b/d with US military escort, the UAE restored 3.9mn b/d of exports, Iran has shipped 40mn+ barrels since the naval blockade lifted, and Russian exports are at records. Transmission: falling oil -> eroding energy-CPI floors -> less CB hawkishness at the margin -> and the currency holding the oil bag is CAD (weakest, USD/CAD ~1.42, a 14-mo low). Falling oil is also the GER40 tailwind, softened the RBNZ's cost-shock case (part of why Jul 8 flipped to a hold), and does NOT buy a Fed cut - US disinflation is energy-led while core runs 2.9%. The tail is funeral week itself: talks frozen, emotions high, Iran's Jul 2 Hormuz warning on the record, and Tehran still demanding maritime control of the strait - a fresh incident with negotiators sidelined would re-spike oil into a market that has fully crushed the war premium (rescuing CAD, hurting AUD/NZD/GER40, handing USD and JPY competing haven bids). With floating storage high, though, even a spike likely fades faster than prior episodes.
